messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: AgentHost: local AH sessions don't show progress in vscode ISSUE BODY: No progress indicator in the chat list. And if I click away, they seem to be cancelled. Works fine in agents window. --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Instructions 1. **Always read the `sessions` skill first.** This is your primary source of truth for the sessions architecture. - Invoke `skill: "sessions"`. 2. Focus your work on `src/vs/sessions/`. 3. Avoid making changes to core VS Code files (`src/vs/workbench/`, `src/vs/platform/`, etc.) unless absolutely necessary for the agents window functionality. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\agentHostTesting.instructions.md ===== --- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/platform/agentHost/**' --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host unit tests For tests in this area that touch the SessionDatabase, they MUST use an in-memory database, not a real database file on disk. Use `SessionDatabase.open(':memory:')` and see the examples from existing tests. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\platform\agentHost\test\auth-rework.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Quick Start with Aspire Dashboard To collect agent host traces with the [Aspire Dashboard](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire/fundamentals/dashboard/standalone) (or any OTLP-compatible collector): ```json { "chat.agentHost.enabled": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.enabled": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.captureContent": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.dbSpanExporter.enabled": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.otlpEndpoint": "http://localhost:4318" } ``` This combination persists every span to SQLite for offline inspection **and** forwards them live to Aspire — useful when you want to spot-check a session in the dashboard and still be able to query the raw data later. --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # ⚠️: Internal use only. To onboard, follow instructions at https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/blob/main/docs/gh-mcp-onboarding.md agent: agent model: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (copilot) argument-hint: Describe your issue. Include relevant keywords or phrases. description: Search for an existing VS Code GitHub issue tools: - github/* - agent/runSubagent --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### `../../chatSessions/vscode-node/chatHistoryBuilder.ts` Converts a persisted `IClaudeCodeSession` into VS Code `ChatResponsePart[]` for replay in the chat UI: - Reconstructs assistant text, thinking blocks, tool invocations, and tool results into chat response parts - Matches subagent sessions to their spawning Agent/Task tool_use blocks using `ISubagentSession.parentToolUseId`, injecting the subagent's tool calls inline under the parent tool invocation

response:
```json
{
  "title": "AgentHost: Fix local AH session progress indicators in VS Code chat list",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "vscode-integration", "ui-bug", "sessions"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code user running local AgentHost, I want to see progress indicators for active sessions in the chat list so that I can monitor ongoing agent work without switching views.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an active AgentHost session is running, When viewing the chat list in VS Code, Then it displays a progress indicator (e.g., 'Running...', loading spinner) instead of empty state",
    "Given multiple AgentHost sessions are running concurrently, When viewing the chat list, Then each session shows its individual progress status",
    "Given I click away from the chat list view, When returning to the chat list, Then active AgentHost sessions remain visible with their progress indicators intact (not cancelled)",
    "Given a completed AgentHost session finishes execution, When viewing the chat list, Then it displays as completed/finalized rather than running",
    "The behavior matches what is shown in the agents window for comparison"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate session persistence mechanism - verify if local AH sessions are being cleared on view change",
      "Review chatHistoryBuilder.ts to ensure progress state is included in VS Code ChatResponsePart[] conversion",
      "Add session metadata tracking for 'isRunning' or similar status field",
      "Ensure SessionDatabase properly persists and reloads running session state across view changes"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update chat list UI component to render progress indicators for AgentHost sessions",
      "Add visual feedback (spinner, status text) for in-progress sessions",
      "Implement proper session state management when switching between views"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Verify progress indicators appear for all running local AH sessions",
      "Test that clicking away and returning preserves active session states",
      "Compare behavior with agents window to ensure consistency",
      "Test concurrent multiple agent sessions display correctly"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Progress indicators visible in chat list for all running AgentHost sessions",
    "Sessions persist state across view changes (click away and return)",
    "Code reviewed by backend and frontend team members",
    "All acceptance criteria tests passed",
    "No regressions in existing agents window functionality"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected format for progress indicators? (spinner, status text, both?)",
    "Are there any specific session states that should show different indicators?",
    "Should we track which AgentHost instance each session belongs to?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The issue is specifically with local AgentHost sessions vs agents window sessions",
    "Session data exists but isn't being properly converted/refreshed for the chat list view",
    "The root cause is either missing conversion logic or session state not persisting across views"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host unit tests section",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - chatSessions/vscode-node/chatHistoryBuilder.ts documentation",
    "AgentHost testing instructions"
  ]
}
```
